phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Q from fog-of-war range on his every last-hit — level 1 Xerath is the longest gun in the lane. Mana is the only governor.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: W joins the pattern: Q-W on the slowed target is the chip combo that defines the next twenty minutes.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: E completes the kill kit: the stun into full rotation is real damage even now. Hold it for HIS aggression, not yours.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Lost Chapter timing — the artillery goes infinite. Poke the wave contact, the trade attempt, and the recall walk.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Rite of the Arcane online — chipped targets anywhere in the lane's zip code are now finishable. Shove and snipe.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Luden's spike: Q-W chunks third-bars. He farms at half HP forever or he doesn't farm.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Late Xerath wins fights from the next postal code: stun the entry, barrage the objective, and never be seen.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Respect window: at two-plus items the stardust ramp control beat your long-range mana poke cleanly. Trade only off Aurelion Sol's cooldowns and keep the wave where the lead can't cash out.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Your long-range mana poke out-muscle Azir's soldier-poke shuffle control at level 1 — this window is yours, so force the trades and tax every step toward the wave.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: At level 2 your long-range mana poke beat the soldier-poke shuffle control in a straight exchange. Play the front foot — make Azir answer you, not the other way around.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: Your long-range mana poke out-muscle Azir's soldier-poke shuffle control at level 3 — this window is yours, so force the trades and tax every step toward the wave.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Through levels 4-5 your long-range mana poke beat the soldier-poke shuffle control in a straight exchange. Play the front foot — make Azir answer you, not the other way around.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Slight edge at the level-6 breakpoint: your long-range mana poke beat Azir's soldier-poke shuffle control on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Luden's spike: Q-W chunks third-bars. He farms at half HP forever or he doesn't farm.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Azir's soldier-poke shuffle control own this window — your long-range mana poke don't answer them at two-plus items yet. Farm what's safe and let the next spike re-price the lane.
